records.title: Diamond Bar High School — Overview
records.summary: Diamond Bar High School (DBHS) is a comprehensive public high school in Diamond Bar, California, serving grades 9–12 as part of the Walnut Valley Unified School District. It opened in 1982 and enrolls approximately 2,600 students. The school's mascot is the Brahma Bull and colors are purple and gold.

records.title: DBHS History and Community Demographics
records.summary: Diamond Bar High School opened in 1982 in Diamond Bar, California. Diamond Bar is a multi-ethnic, upper-middle-class community along the 57 and 60 freeways on the eastern edge of Los Angeles County. DBHS is one of two comprehensive high schools in WVUSD. The city's population was 53,857 in 2021 with a median income of $100,730. Over 50% of DBHS student households speak a language other than English at home.

facts.location_description: Along the 57 and 60 freeways, eastern edge of Los Angeles County
facts.district_also_has: 1 continuation high school, 3 middle schools, 9 elementary schools
facts.student_demographics: Over 50% of students speak a language other than English at home

records.title: DBHS Vision, Mission, and Student Learning Outcomes (SLOs)
records.summary: DBHS's vision is to be a premier school committed to the intellectual, physical, and social development of every student. The mission centers on the five A's — Academics, Access, Activities, Arts, and Athletics — so each student receives a personalized educational program from a rigorous curriculum.
records.vision_statement: Diamond Bar High School will be a premier school committed to the intellectual, physical, and social development of every student. Diamond Bar will be a community where every student's cultural heritage enriches the learning experience for all students.
records.mission_statement: Diamond Bar will value the five A's of Academics, Access, Activities, Arts, and Athletics so that each student will experience a personalized educational program selected from a rigorous, rich and varied curriculum. Students will be challenged to discover their unique gifts and talents; staff members will support every student in realizing his or her potential. Working in partnership with parents and the community, the Diamond Bar High School staff will provide learning opportunities that reach beyond the boundaries of the classroom and ensure every student a chance for success in college and career, and as citizens and leaders of the 21st century.

records.title: WASC Accreditation
records.summary: DBHS is accredited by the Western Association of Schools and Colleges (WASC), a 501(c)(3) organization recognized as one of six regional associations that accredit public and private schools in the United States. DBHS has published multiple self-study reports and visiting committee reports.
keywords: Western Association of Schools and Colleges

records.title: DBHS Awards and US News Rankings 2024–25
records.summary: DBHS has been ranked by US News & World Report as one of the best high schools in the nation every year from 2008 through 2024. In the 2024–25 rankings it placed #408 nationally, #56 in California, #21 in the Los Angeles metro area, and #149 in STEM schools.

rankings_2024_25.los_angeles_metro: #21 in Los Angeles, CA Metro Area High Schools

ranking_factors.description: Proportion of 12th graders who took and earned a qualifying score on AP or IB exams (CRI).
Percentage of 12th graders who took and earned qualifying scores on multiple AP or IB exams in 4 content areas.
Performance on state standardized tests in math, reading and science.
Total assessment scores compared with predicted scores for a school with similar demographics.
Learning outcomes among Black, Hispanic and low-income students vs. non-underserved students.
Proportion of students who entered 9th grade in 2018–2019 who graduated by 2022.

records.title: How to Enroll at DBHS
records.summary: Enrollment for DBHS residents for the 2026–27 school year begins January 19, 2026. Students must live within the DBHS attendance area. The online application is submitted through the WVUSD Aeries enrollment portal.
process_steps: Confirm your address falls within the DBHS enrollment area
Complete the online application at the WVUSD Aeries portal
School will contact families about additional documents and appointment to finalize enrollment

records.title: Enrollment Documents Required — Resident Students
records.summary: Students living with parents must submit eight documents to complete DBHS enrollment, including proof of residency, immunization records, and student identification.
required_documents: 2. DMV-issued driver's license or California ID with current address
3. Current utility bill or utility turn-on receipt (gas, water, or electric) for current address
4. Close-of-escrow paper, current monthly mortgage statement, current year's property tax statement, or current rental/lease agreement
5. Home Language Survey (also available in Mandarin)
7. Student Proof of Age (birth certificate or passport)
records.guardian_additional: Proof of legal guardianship (court order) OR a completed Caregiver's Authorization Affidavit and Additional Authorizations for Caregiver Form
records.shared_housing_additional: Verification of Residency Part B AND a DMV-issued ID with current address
additional_docs_us_students: Official Transcript(s) or 8th grade report card for incoming 9th graders
additional_docs_international: Official Transcript(s) or 8th grade report card for incoming 9th graders

records.title: Grade-Level Placement by Birthdate for 2026–27 School Year
records.summary: Grade placement for DBHS 2026–27 is determined by student birthdate ranges.

records.title: Myoung Han — Registrar
records.summary: Mrs. Myoung Han is the DBHS Registrar. She handles enrollment, transcripts, and student records. Contact her if a Parchment transcript request is not processed within 2 business days.
records.name: Myoung Han

records.title: Janna Van Horn — Activities Director
records.summary: Mrs. Janna Van Horn is the DBHS Activities Director and oversees the United Student Body (USB). USB executive board candidates must first be approved by Mrs. Van Horn before running for election.
records.name: Janna Van Horn

records.title: Jennifer Burns — AP Coordinator
records.summary: Jennifer Burns is the DBHS Advanced Placement (AP) Coordinator. She handles AP Classroom registration, exam payments, and AP-related logistics. Students can email her if their AP Classroom period assignment is incorrect.

records.email_note: Email Jennifer Burns for AP Classroom section corrections

records.title: Margaret Ku — IB Program Contact
records.summary: Margaret Ku is the DBHS IB program contact. Donations to support IB student activities, social events, and classroom supplies should be sent to her attention.
records.name: Margaret Ku
records.mailing_address: Diamond Bar High School, Attention: Margaret Ku, 21400 East Pathfinder Road, Diamond Bar, CA 91765

records.title: Barbara Pentangelo — Brahma Tech Academy Coordinator
records.summary: Barbara Pentangelo is one of two coordinators for the Brahma Tech Academy (PLTW/Engineering program) at DBHS.

records.title: Jennifer Chung — Attendance Clerk (Students Lio–Z)

records.title: Ms. Ayala — Career Center Coordinator
records.summary: Ms. Ayala works in the College and Career Center, located inside the Express Library (Room 265). She processes work permit applications (1–2 business days). Students can schedule appointments via Calendly.
records.name: Ms. Ayala

records.title: Grade Level Coordinators (GLC) — Overview
records.summary: Grade Level Coordinators (GLCs) act as parental figures on campus. Each grade level has two GLCs who help with scheduling, class selection, college admission guidance, conflict resolution, and personal support. GLCs are visible on campus during Brunch and Lunch and are available for student drop-ins.
records.role_description: GLCs act as parents on campus. They handle conflicts, assist with class selection, explain college admission policies, and provide personal guidance. They are out on campus during Brunch and Lunch.
records.contact_method: Approach during Brunch or Lunch, or contact through school

records.title: Pathways Academy Coordinator
records.summary: Ms. Brummett serves as the Grade Level Coordinator for the Pathways Communications Academy at DBHS.

records.title: Class of 2026 Grade Level Coordinators
records.summary: Mr. Desmond and Mrs. Tumbeiro are the Grade Level Coordinators for the Class of 2026 (current seniors). They assist with scheduling, college applications, and personal guidance.

records.title: Class of 2027 Grade Level Coordinators
records.summary: Mr. Gonzales and Mrs. Burns are the Grade Level Coordinators for the Class of 2027 (current juniors). They assist with scheduling, college preparation, and personal guidance.

records.title: Class of 2028 Grade Level Coordinators
records.summary: Ms. Lee and Mrs. Zimmerman are the Grade Level Coordinators for the Class of 2028 (current sophomores).

records.title: Class of 2029 Grade Level Coordinators
records.summary: Mr. Galeener and Mrs. Duenas are the Grade Level Coordinators for the Class of 2029 (current freshmen).

records.title: Class of 2030 Grade Level Coordinators
records.summary: Mr. Desmond and Ms. Brummett are the Grade Level Coordinators for the Class of 2030 (incoming freshmen).

records.title: Free Peer Tutoring — CSF (California Scholastic Federation)
records.summary: DBHS offers free peer tutoring provided by CSF students in any subject. After-school sessions meet Monday through Thursday in Room 655, and before-school sessions meet Tuesday and Thursday in Room 656. No cost to students.

records.title: Peer Tutoring For a Fee
records.summary: A list of fee-based peer tutors (typically Honors and AP-level students) is available from a student's GLC. Tutoring may take place on or off campus and costs vary.
records.how_to_find: Request the tutoring list from your Grade Level Coordinator (GLC)

records.title: Math and Science Tutoring — Mt. San Antonio College High School Referral Program
records.summary: Mt. SAC offers free tutoring in Pre-Algebra, Algebra 1, Algebra 2, Geometry, Biology, and Chemistry through the High School Referral Program. Walk-ins are welcome at Building 30, Room 115.

records.title: LA County Library Online Tutoring Program
records.summary: LA County Library offers one-on-one online tutoring via the internet at no cost.

records.title: College and Career Center
records.summary: The DBHS College and Career Center, located in the Express Library (Room 265) and run by Ms. Ayala, helps students with college planning, career exploration, work permits, financial aid, scholarships, and testing. Students can book appointments via Calendly.

records.title: Work Permits — How to Apply
records.summary: California law requires students under 18 to have a work permit on file with the school and their employer. Work permits are processed by Ms. Ayala in the Career Center (Express Library, Room 265) and take 1–2 business days. Permits must be renewed annually.
records.legal_requirement: Students under age 18 must have a work permit. It is required by California law.
records.where_to_apply: Ms. Ayala, Career Center, Express Library, Room 265
records.renewal: Must be renewed each year if under 18
steps: 1. Once hired by an employer, complete the Minor's Information section
3. Have a parent or legal guardian sign the application
4. Have the employer complete and sign the Employer Information section
5. Turn in the completed form to Ms. Ayala in the Career Center (Room 265)

records.title: Requesting Official Transcripts — Parchment
records.summary: Current and former DBHS students request official transcripts through Parchment (powered by Docufide). Transcripts can be sent to universities, scholarship foundations, and the NCAA. Seniors must personally request their final transcript to be sent to their college — DBHS does not do this automatically.
registration_requirements: Use only ONE email address per student
records.processing_contact: Mrs. Han (Registrar) — contact if not processed within 2 business days
records.senior_note: Final transcripts are NOT sent automatically. Seniors must personally request their final transcript through Parchment to be sent to their attending university.

records.title: Advanced Placement (AP) Program
records.summary: DBHS offers a robust AP program coordinated by Jennifer Burns. Students register in AP Classroom (via teacher join codes) and pay for exams through the DBHS Webstore. Important deadlines govern payment, cancellation, and fee-reduction forms.
records.registration: Log into AP Classroom using teacher-provided join code; use personal email and legal name
records.payment: Pay for exam through DBHS Webstore after registering in AP Classroom

key_dates.event: Last day to turn in AP Fee Reduction Forms (Room 557)
Last day to pay for AP exams (yearlong and Fall semester classes); $40 late fee and $40 cancellation fee apply after this date
Last day to pay for Spring-only semester classes; last day to cancel with $40 fee; last day to order with $40 late fee; no refund after this date

records.exam_window: AP Exams: May 5–16 (Late Start Schedule on Wednesday, May 14)

records.title: International Baccalaureate (IB) Diploma Program — Overview
records.summary: DBHS offers the IB Diploma Program (DP), a challenging two-year curriculum for students aged 16–19 that leads to a globally recognized qualification. The program encourages inquiry, intercultural understanding, and lifelong learning. IB contact is Margaret Ku.
records.mailing_address: Diamond Bar High School, Attention: Margaret Ku, 21400 East Pathfinder Road, Diamond Bar, CA 91765
goals: Develop inquiring, knowledgeable, caring young people
Prepare students for leading universities worldwide

records.title: IB Courses — Group 1: Literature
records.summary: IB Group 1 (Literature) courses at DBHS are offered at Higher Level (HL) only. Both courses carry UC honors status.

records.title: IB Courses — Group 2: Second Language
records.summary: IB Group 2 (Second Language) at DBHS includes Spanish, Mandarin, French, Korean, and Language B ab initio at HL and SL levels.

courses.name: Language B ab initio IB SL
courses.note: May only be taken as a Group 6 subject, in addition to another Group 2 language

records.title: IB Courses — Group 3: Individuals and Societies
records.summary: IB Group 3 (Individuals and Societies) at DBHS includes History HL/SL and Environmental Systems and Societies SL.

courses.name: History of the Americas IB HL2
Modern American History (1st semester) / History IB SL (2nd semester)
Environmental Systems and Societies IB SL

records.title: IB Courses — Group 4: Natural/Laboratory Sciences
records.summary: IB Group 4 (Sciences) at DBHS includes Biology, Physics, and Chemistry at both HL and SL levels.

courses.name: Environmental Systems and Societies IB SL

records.title: IB Courses — Group 5: Mathematics
records.summary: IB Group 5 (Mathematics) at DBHS includes Analysis and Approaches at HL (equivalent to AP Calculus II) and Applications and Interpretation at SL.

courses.name: Mathematics: Analysis and Approaches IB HL1
Mathematics: Analysis and Approaches IB HL2
Mathematics: Applications and Interpretation IB SL

records.title: IB Courses — Group 6: Arts and Electives
records.summary: IB Group 6 (Arts) at DBHS includes Visual Arts, Music, and Theatre at HL and SL levels.

records.title: IB Diploma Requirements
records.summary: To earn the IB Diploma at DBHS, students must complete six subjects (one from each group), take at least 3 at Higher Level, complete an Extended Essay, Theory of Knowledge, and CAS (Creativity, Activity, Service). Minimum 3.0 GPA is required to enter and remain in the IB program.
records.subject_requirements: One course from each of the 6 IB subject groups
records.level_requirements: 3 subjects at HL and 3 at SL (or 4 HL and 2 SL). A student may only take a maximum of 2 SL courses in junior year.

core_elements.description: A senior seminar course connecting all disciplines
Creativity, Activity, and Service — requires meeting 7 learning outcomes with CAS projects

gpa_requirements.freshmen_sophomores: Maintain 3.0 or higher cumulative GPA in Groups 1–5 courses (reviewed every semester)
gpa_requirements.junior_entry: Sophomores wishing to continue must formally declare intent in early March. Students with 3.0+ GPA in IB subject groups 1–5 and who meet prerequisites automatically continue. Students below 3.0 or missing prerequisites cannot continue.

records.group_6_substitution: A second subject from Groups 3 or 4 may substitute for a Group 6 subject
records.ib_course_candidate: A student may take individual IB courses and sit for exams without pursuing the full diploma. Passing IB assessments may earn college credit.
graduation_requirements_not_covered_by_i: Physical Education: 4 semesters during grades 9 and 10
Social Science: US History (1 year), Civics (1 semester), Economics (1 semester)
Applied Arts: 3 semesters including a course demonstrating computer literacy
UC/CSU: 1-year course in visual or performing arts

records.title: Brahma Tech Academy (PLTW / Engineering)
records.summary: The Brahma Tech Academy is a 4-year STEM program where students develop STEM identity, expertise, and leadership. The program culminates in a 150-hour internship. Graduates receive a STEM certificate and wear a stole at graduation. Coordinators are Barbara Pentangelo and Jeremiah Wai.
records.completion_award: Brahma Tech STEM certificate; academy stole at graduation

records.title: Career Technical Education (CTE) and ROP
records.summary: DBHS offers Career Technical Education (CTE) pathways and ROP (Regional Occupational Program) courses. The school operates a Print3D Works! online store. CTE details and pathway descriptions are primarily in linked PDFs and the Career Center.
resources: Walnut Valley USD Labor Market PDF
records.college_career_center_link: See College and Career Center for additional CTE/ROP info

records.title: DBHS Mathematics Department
records.summary: DBHS Mathematics ('Brahma Mathematics') has won regional and national math competitions including California Mathematics League Regional Championships (2011, 2013, 2014, 2016, 2022) and AP Calculus World Championships (2005, 2007). Students requesting recommendation letters from math teachers must submit requests at least 3 weeks before the deadline and check teacher-specific requirements 5 weeks in advance.
achievements: 2007–2008 Siemens Award for Advanced Placement winner
records.recommendation_letter_policy: Submit request to math teacher NO LATER than 3 weeks before application due date. Check teacher-specific requirements at least 5 weeks prior.

records.title: Chinese Language Department — Placement Tests
records.summary: DBHS offers Chinese language courses at levels C2/2H, C3/3H, AP, and IB. Placement tests are required for incoming students. Tests are held at different locations for different middle schools. Results are posted after February 24, 2026. Contact Mrs. Lu and Mr. Wu for more information.

placement_test_dates_2026.for: South Pointe Middle School students only
DBHS, Suzanne Middle School, or other district students only

records.registration: Complete the CPT online registration form before assigned testing date
records.review_materials: AP/IB vocabulary review (45 pages; study 4–5 pages per week; Traditional and Simplified PDF versions)

records.title: English Learner Program / ELAC
records.summary: The DBHS English Learner Program provides support for English learner students and is connected to the English Learner Advisory Committee (ELAC). The DELAC Representative is Mr. Nan Jiang. An ELD Resource Hub is available online.

records.title: DBHS Graduation Requirements
records.summary: All DBHS students must complete specific graduation requirements. The full authoritative list is in the Course Description Book (PDF). Key requirements include Physical Education, Social Science, Health, Applied Arts, and a visual/performing arts course for UC/CSU entrance.

requirements.subject: Social Science
requirements.requirement: US History (1 year), Civics (1 semester), Economics (1 semester)

requirements.subject: Applied Arts
requirements.requirement: 3 semesters of vocational or creative art; must include a course demonstrating computer literacy

requirements.subject: Visual/Performing Arts (UC/CSU)
requirements.requirement: 1-year course in the visual or performing arts

records.full_requirements: Complete, authoritative graduation matrix in DBHS Course Description Book (PDF)
records.sat_note: Use CEEB Code 050748 when registering for the SAT on the College Board website

records.title: DBHS Arts Programs
records.summary: DBHS offers a wide range of performing and visual arts programs including Choir, Commercial Music Band, Dance, Drama, Music and Colorguard, Pep, and the Performing Arts Academy.

records.title: United Student Body (USB) — Overview
records.summary: USB (United Student Body) is the student government at DBHS, chosen over 'ASB' at the 1982 student body convention. USB has 6 executive board members and 34 commissioners. Members serve January–December and are enrolled in 4th period Leadership class. USB manages lunchtime activities, rallies, dances, the Student Store, and club approvals.
records.founded_name: USB chosen over ASB at 1982 student body convention
records.how_to_join: E-board candidates approved by Mrs. Van Horn then elected; commissioners selected by e-board via paper application and two interviews

records.title: USB Executive Board 2025
records.summary: The 2025 USB Executive Board consists of six elected officers led by President Miles Lai.

records.title: USB Commissioners 2025
records.summary: The 2025 USB has 34 commissioners across 16 commissions handling academics, advertising, art, athletics, audio, hospitality, performing arts, social activities, spirit, student store, technology, and more.

records.title: USB Executive Board 2024 (Archived)
records.summary: The 2024 USB Executive Board was led by President Jane Kim.

records.title: DBHS Athletics Overview
records.summary: DBHS athletics are organized through CIF-SS (school ID 139) and compete in the Hacienda League (football) and Palomares League (all other sports). Select events are live-streamed on NFHS Network. Athletic clearance must be submitted online before participation.

records.title: Regular Day Bell Schedule (Monday, Tuesday, Thursday, Friday)
records.summary: On regular school days (Mon, Tue, Thu, Fri), DBHS runs 7 periods. School starts at 8:30 AM for Period 1 and ends at 3:30 PM for Period 6. Brunch is at 10:30 AM and Lunch at 12:48 PM.
records.notes: Periods 1A and 6A are for AP Science classes only

records.title: Wednesday Late Start Bell Schedule
records.summary: Every Wednesday DBHS has a late start. School begins at 9:15 AM for Period 1 (after a Late Start at 8:30 AM) and ends at 3:30 PM. No Zero Period or Period 1A Science Lab on Wednesdays.
records.notes: No Zero Period or 1A Science Lab on Wednesdays

records.title: Minimum Day Bell Schedule
records.summary: On Minimum Days, all periods are 30 minutes. School starts at 8:30 AM for Period 1 and ends at 12:10 PM for Period 6.

records.title: Final Exam Bell Schedule (3 Days)
records.summary: Finals span 3 days. Day 1: Periods 0, 3, 6. Day 2: Periods 1, 2. Day 3: Periods 4, 5. Each final exam is 120 minutes (AP Science finals are 155 minutes).

records.title: Summer School Bell Schedule
records.summary: Summer school runs two periods per day. Period 1 is 7:30–10:00 AM. Period 2 (WVUSD/D2L) runs 10:30 AM–1:00 PM; Mt. SAC classes run until 1:30 PM.

records.title: Pathways Academy Bell Schedule — Regular Day
records.summary: Pathways Academy students follow a block schedule on regular days (Mon, Tue, Thu, Fri). Blocks 7 and 8 replace Periods 1–3. Period 4 starts at 11:52 AM.

records.title: Key Dates — 2026 School Calendar
records.summary: Key dates for DBHS 2025–26 school year including summer school weeks, first day of school, and major holidays.

key_dates.event: Summer School Week 3 — End of 1st Semester
Summer School Week 6 — End of 2nd Semester

records.title: Summer School 2026 — Overview
records.summary: DBHS Summer School 2026 runs June 8 – July 16, 2026, Monday–Thursday each week. Classes are held 7:30 AM – 1:00 PM on campus (Mt. SAC classes run until 1:30 PM). Three programs are offered: WVUSD non-tuition remediation, Mt. SAC non-tuition advancement, and D2L tuition-based enrichment.

programs.name: WVUSD Non-Tuition Remediation
programs.description: For students needing to retake a course for graduation credits. Class rosters generated by the student's GLC. For current 9th, 10th, and 11th graders.

programs.name: Mt. SAC Non-Tuition Courses
programs.description: For advancement or remediation in select subject areas. Limited seats via lottery. Priority: current 11th, 10th, 9th graders, then incoming 9th graders (current 8th grade).

programs.name: Dedicated to Learning (D2L) Tuition Program
programs.description: Tuition-based classes for subject advancement. See D2L website for details.

records.title: Attendance Office — Procedures and Contacts
records.summary: The DBHS Attendance Office handles absences, tardies, and early releases. Absences must be cleared within 3 days; tardies within 1 day. Students are assigned to one of two clerks based on last name. The absence line is (909) 594-1860.
how_to_report: Email — include student's full name, student ID number, grade level, date(s) of absence, and reason
Note — sent with student; include full name, ID number, grade, date(s), reason, and parent signature
Call the attendance office clerk directly
records.voicemail_info: Leave: student's full name, student ID number, date of absence/tardy/early leave, and specific reason
records.extended_absence: For 3–14 consecutive days, students may use a short-term independent studies contract with GLC approval

records.title: Health Office — Services and Contacts
records.summary: The DBHS Health Office is staffed by School Nurse Theresa A. Guest (MSN, RN, NCSN) and Health Clerk Evelyn Maldonado. The office handles immunization records, medication consent, and student health assessment forms.
records.immunization_law: California law (17 CCR section 6065) requires immunization records for all TK/K–12 students before admission
immunization_record_must_show: Student name and date of birth
Month, date, and year each vaccine was received
Name of physician or agency who gave vaccine
records.medication_policy: California Education Code 49423 allows school nurse or designated staff to assist students taking medication during school hours. Download Medication Required During School Consent form.
forms_available: Medication Required During School Hours Consent
Parents Guide Immunizations (English, Chinese, Korean, Spanish, Vietnamese)

records.title: DBHS Wellness Center
records.summary: The DBHS Wellness Center (Room 254, opened January 2016) is a confidential, nonjudgmental space for students seeking wellness support. Peer counselors provide guidance for personal well-being. Therapy dog 'Daisy' visits Mondays and Thursdays. Open during Periods 1–4 as a drop-in.
records.mission: Committed to creating a supportive environment by providing wellness education and resources. Peer counselors guide students in developing healthy personal well-being strategies.

records.title: Parent Groups — The Magnificent Seven
records.summary: DBHS has seven parent support organizations collectively known as the 'Magnificent Seven.' They provide essential financial and volunteer support to the school.

groups.name: Hispanic Organization for Parents and Education
Parents of India and Neighboring Countries

records.title: Student Fees Policy — Free Public Education
records.summary: The California Constitution mandates that public education be provided free of charge. DBHS cannot require students to pay for educational activities, materials, supplies, or uniforms, whether curricular or extracurricular. A waiver process for a mandatory fee does not make the fee constitutional.
records.legal_basis: California Constitution — mandates free public education
records.scope: All educational activities, curricular or extracurricular, with or without credit
prohibits: Mandatory fees for programs or activities
Mandatory purchase of materials, supplies, equipment, or uniforms
Security deposits for access, participation, materials, or equipment

records.title: DBHS Nondiscrimination Policy
records.summary: DBHS prohibits discrimination, harassment, intimidation, or bullying in all district programs, activities, and employment based on a wide range of actual or perceived characteristics.
protected_characteristics: association with a person or group with one or more of these characteristics
records.scope: All district programs, activities, and employment

records.title: Bullying Prevention Policy
records.summary: DBHS has an anti-bullying policy and provides resources including an Anti-Bullying Information PDF, a Cyber Safety Guide, and links to StopBullying.gov.

records.title: Suicide Prevention Resources
records.summary: DBHS provides suicide prevention resources including the 988 Suicide and Crisis Lifeline and Crisis Text Line. Students and families can call or text 988 for immediate support.

records.title: Care Solace / Mental Health Crisis Resources
records.summary: DBHS provides Care Solace resources and the LA County ACCESS hotline for mental health emergencies. The LA County hotline operates 24/7 and provides crisis evaluation teams, information, referrals, and interpreter services.

records.title: Fentanyl / Drug Awareness
records.summary: DBHS provides fentanyl and drug awareness resources through the LA County Office of Education, including videos, parent guides, and information on overdose signs and symptoms.

records.title: Mindfulness Room
records.summary: DBHS has a Mindfulness Room available to students as a wellness resource.

records.title: DBHS Distinguished Alumni
records.summary: DBHS has produced numerous distinguished alumni across sports, entertainment, business, and public service.

alumni.name: Alex Morgan
alumni.notes: Professional soccer player (US Women's National Team)

records.title: Important Links and External Systems
records.summary: Key online systems and portals used by DBHS students, parents, and staff.

records.summary: DBHS Digital Video Production and PCA Sophomore students attended the Directing Change Awards Ceremony at the United Theatre in Los Angeles in June 2026. Students created stop-motion animations for the Directing Change Film Contest and attended the CDOL Student Empowerment Conference at AMC Universal City Walk.

partners.description: LA Promise Fund initiative delivering media arts-integrated education

partners.name: CDOL (Center of Distance and Online Learning)

records.summary: Abby Kottke (DBHS Class of 2022, CTE Digital Video Production pathway completer and former Brahma Cinematics Club President) graduated from CSULB's Cinematic Arts program and presented films at the Full Spectrum 2026 Cinematic Showcase. She received a $500 scholarship for her Eye Opener documentary.

films.title: RIICHI REACH

records.title: DBHS CEEB / College Board Code
records.summary: The Diamond Bar High School CEEB code (College Board code) is 050748. Students need this code to register for the SAT, ACT, AP exams, and to complete college applications.

records.title: Pathways Communications Academy
records.summary: The Pathways Communications Academy at DBHS is funded by a California Partnership Academies state grant. It is one of the specialized academy programs at DBHS. The GLC for the Pathways Academy is Ms. Brummett. Students follow a block schedule (Blocks 7 and 8) instead of traditional Periods 1–3.

records.title: Student Clubs and Organizations
records.summary: DBHS has a large number of active student clubs for the 2025–26 school year. The official roster is maintained by USB. Jeremiah Wai updates the reference list. The full club list (with names, advisors, and meeting details) is in an embedded Google Sheet.
records.note: Clubs must be approved and maintain good standing with USB. Charter and renewal requirements apply.

records.title: Principal's Newsletter (Smore)
records.summary: The DBHS Principal's Newsletter is published weekly via Smore. Recent editions include Weeks 32–38 from April–May 2026.
